items.name: Build the Coach Dashboard
items.why: A team-first view: matchup roll-ups, per-player skill breakdowns and player values, all from data we already have.

items.name: Build the front-office manager dashboard
items.why: Refresh who is available to play

items.name: Let long board sections start collapsed
items.header: Let long board sections start collapsed
items.why: Depth charts and other detail open on a tap, so the board reads at a glance with detail one click away.

items.name: Build one shared player-and-team value backbone
items.header: Build one shared player-and-team value backbone
items.why: A single source for player worth and team strength so the coach and manager views can never disagree.

items.name: Smell-test fixes: shooting luck, bench context, thin-sample regression
items.header: Smell-test fixes: shooting luck, bench context, thin-sample regression
items.why: Separate real skill from luck and pull tiny samples toward a sensible baseline.

items.name: Turn social-media injury signals into live prediction inputs
items.header: Turn social-media injury signals into live prediction inputs
items.why: The collector is built; put it on a schedule and wire it into the forecasts.

items.name: Add an official injury feed and learn from past news signals
items.header: Add an official injury feed and learn from past news signals
items.why: So social posts aren't our only source for who is playing tonight.

items.name: Wire the finished foul engine into the live game simulation
items.header: Wire the finished foul engine into the live game simulation
items.why: The foul-trouble engine passed its tests but nothing reads it yet; build that connection, then turn it on so fouls shape scoring like they do in real games.

items.name: Build a usage-and-budget report for the automated work
items.header: Build a usage-and-budget report for the automated work
items.why: See spend at a glance and re-slot the model-building work against it.

items.name: Recover and re-run a request if a session crashes mid-work
items.header: Recover and re-run a request if a session crashes mid-work
items.why: A low-priority safety net so a crash never quietly drops your message.

infra.name: Stop cutting off long messages when they are captured
infra.header: Stop cutting off long messages when they are captured
infra.why: Raise the capture limit so no request tail is ever lost at intake again.

infra.name: Let batched answers wait for the morning digest without churning
infra.header: Let batched answers wait for the morning digest without churning
infra.why: A non-blocking state so the reminder system does not thrash on a held answer.

infra.name: Only spin up extra work lanes when the next item clears a value bar
infra.header: Only spin up extra work lanes when the next item clears a value bar
infra.why: Stops overnight sessions from over-spending on low-value tail work.

infra.name: Reserve the heavy independent double-check for work that ships
infra.header: Reserve the heavy independent double-check for work that ships
infra.why: Dead-end results get a light self-check; save the deep check for live changes.

infra.name: Always merge onto the latest code, never blind-copy a work branch
infra.header: Always merge onto the latest code, never blind-copy a work branch
infra.why: Prevents a stale branch from silently reverting a live feature.

infra.name: One-time code-publishing sign-in to keep pushes flowing
infra.header: One-time code-publishing sign-in to keep pushes flowing
infra.why: Needs a human sign-in; mostly self-heals now, with a small residual.

infra.name: Send a phone ping when real work ships, and prove it lands
infra.header: Send a phone ping when real work ships, and prove it lands
infra.why: Hear about finished deliverables automatically, with a daily catch-up for anything big.

infra.name: Schedule the prediction-range builder
infra.why: It has no schedule and last ran a week ago; wire it so reports stop showing blanks.

infra.name: Move leftover notebooks into automatic jobs
infra.header: Move leftover notebooks into automatic jobs
infra.why: Retire the last manual notebooks to kill stale-password and drift failures.

modeling.name: Guard against a broken rating build burying a star player
modeling.header: Guard against a broken rating build burying a star player
modeling.why: Add a safety check so an empty rating build can never hide the best players.

modeling.name: Point displayed prediction ranges at the better-tuned widths
modeling.header: Point displayed prediction ranges at the better-tuned widths
modeling.why: So our stated confidence lines up with how often we are actually right.

modeling.name: Correct the remaining teams we still rate too high or too low
modeling.header: Correct the remaining teams we still rate too high or too low
modeling.why: Dallas and Las Vegas read too low, Los Angeles too high; extend the shipped fix.

modeling.name: Make the win-percent and the point-spread tell the same story
modeling.header: Make the win-percent and the point-spread tell the same story
modeling.why: They currently disagree by about a point; reconcile the two cleanly.

modeling.name: Stop an automatic tune-up from over-correcting the home-court edge
modeling.header: Stop an automatic tune-up from over-correcting the home-court edge
modeling.why: It is about two games from firming too hard; keep the well-calibrated setting.

modeling.name: Ship the season-long player-impact ranking
modeling.header: Ship the season-long player-impact ranking
modeling.why: A season-long player value view is built and checked; put it live for readers.

modeling.name: Grade our season simulation against the futures market
modeling.header: Grade our season simulation against the futures market
modeling.why: Title, playoff and win-total odds scored vs the market — the sim is the product.

modeling.name: Predict realistic return dates for injured players
modeling.header: Predict realistic return dates for injured players
modeling.why: Refresh who is available to play

modeling.name: Measure player impact by court zone
modeling.header: Measure player impact by court zone
modeling.why: Break down offense and defense value by area of the floor for sharper scouting.

modeling.name: Build the next stage of the game simulator
modeling.header: Build the next stage of the game simulator
modeling.why: The player-attribute foundation for a more realistic possession-by-possession simulation.

modeling.name: Model who guards whom
modeling.header: Model who guards whom
modeling.why: Estimate the likely defender on each play to unlock contest, foul and turnover detail.

modeling.name: Replace hand-set blending numbers with learned ones
modeling.header: Replace hand-set blending numbers with learned ones
modeling.why: Swap every hand-tuned constant for a weight the data re-learns, wherever it earns its keep.

modeling.name: Build a fix for each of our biggest error types
modeling.header: Build a fix for each of our biggest error types
modeling.why: For the ten game-types we predict worst, add the one missing factor that sharpens each.

modeling.name: Measure which player stats are real skill
modeling.header: Measure which player stats are real skill
modeling.why: Find how fast each stat stabilizes so we trust the real ones and calm the noisy ones.

modeling.name: Build player aging curves
modeling.header: Build player aging curves
modeling.why: Estimate how players rise and decline with age to sharpen long-range projections.

modeling.name: Ship the improved minutes-played projections
modeling.header: Ship the improved minutes-played projections
modeling.why: Judge minutes on the games that matter most: players stepping into bigger roles.

modeling.name: Match each prediction range to the game's uncertainty
modeling.header: Match each prediction range to the game's uncertainty
modeling.why: Wider ranges for genuinely uncertain games, tighter for clear ones.

modeling.name: Add a weekly where-we-beat-the-market strip
modeling.header: Add a weekly where-we-beat-the-market strip
modeling.why: A short weekly line showing where our calls beat the market that week.

modeling.name: Test our total-points calls against the market
modeling.header: Test our total-points calls against the market
modeling.why: Check whether our over/under calls beat the market's total when we disagree.

modeling.name: Rank each night's best bets by confidence
modeling.header: Rank each night's best bets by confidence
modeling.why: Combine where we disagree with the market and our track record into a tiered pick list.

data.name: Unify the player-type labels across views
data.header: Unify the player-type labels across views
data.why: Two player-style labelings do not line up; create one shared map so the coach views join cleanly.

data.name: Turn the trade-value ranking into a live feature
data.header: Turn the trade-value ranking into a live feature
data.why: Promote the ranked 169-player trade-value list to the manager view and fix its contract-length and pay gaps.

data.name: Turn on the roster-news collector
data.why: A built collector sits idle at zero rows; every day off loses free early-news signal.

data.name: Capture every player's birthdate
data.why: Ages power aging curves and career projections; free to grab today, harder to backfill later.

data.name: Capture jersey numbers and listed positions
data.header: Capture jersey numbers and listed positions
data.why: Cheap reference data that sharpens roster displays and position priors.

data.name: Capture player headshots
data.why: Portraits make the player pages feel finished; a near-free pull today.

data.name: Refresh the opponent-defense and pace tables
data.header: Refresh the opponent-defense and pace tables
data.why: These stop at last season; rebuild them with this year's games.

data.name: Add college play-by-play with shot locations
data.header: Add college play-by-play with shot locations
data.why: A genuinely new source that unlocks rookie projections we cannot build today.

data.name: Add referee assignments and whistle tendencies
data.header: Add referee assignments and whistle tendencies
data.why: Bring in who officiates each game to study how crews affect foul rates and totals.

data.name: Turn on the defender-matchup data feed
data.header: Turn on the defender-matchup data feed
data.why: The only source we own for who guards whom near the rim; the collector is built and waiting.

data.name: Collect Spanish and French league box scores
data.header: Collect Spanish and French league box scores
data.why: Off-season signal on overseas players, plus an international rookie strength baseline.

data.name: Finish the data-quality guardrails
data.why: Wire the remaining input checks so a bad input is flagged, never silently served.

data.name: Finish the input-quality contracts
data.why: Lock down the last data inputs so a bad date or number cannot slip through unnoticed.

data.name: Collect full 2026 contract terms
data.why: Years, guarantees and options power trade and cap tools; base pay exists but the multi-year terms are missing.

site.name: Build a full in-depth tab for the curious reader
site.header: Build a full in-depth tab for the curious reader
site.why: A richer page that sits above betting and below the top-tier surfaces.

site.name: Add freshness badges and a one-line why to each board row
site.header: Add freshness badges and a one-line why to each board row
site.why: So every row shows how fresh it is and, in a line, why it matters.

site.name: Build the flagship early-morning deep scouting report
site.header: Build the flagship early-morning deep scouting report
site.why: Full roster projections, opponent scouting, rotation reasoning and fair value.

site.name: Fix the last stale-data warnings on the board
site.header: Fix the last stale-data warnings on the board
site.why: Some panels showed hours-old data; add per-panel freshness with a clear flag when a reading is stale.

site.name: Show depth charts and role changes on game cards
site.header: Show depth charts and role changes on game cards
site.why: Who is starting and who is stepping up, right on each game card.

site.name: Add a standings and season-outlook tab
site.header: Add a standings and season-outlook tab
site.why: Projected finish and how each team's season could end, graded against the market.

site.name: Add a we-tested-it-and-it's-noise page
site.why: Publish the ideas we tested and proved dead, to build trust versus tout sites.

site.name: Add descriptive scouting leaderboards
site.why: Built stats like rim protection, clearly labeled scouting rather than predictions.

site.name: Add a head-to-head matchup and player-comparison tool
site.header: Add a head-to-head matchup and player-comparison tool
site.why: Scouting surfaces to compare two teams or two players side by side.

site.name: Bring team pages up to a mainstream sports-site standard
site.header: Bring team pages up to a mainstream sports-site standard
site.why: Fill out the team pages for real product completeness.

site.name: Build the Tempo hub page
site.why: A dedicated team surface and a long-standing target deliverable.

site.name: Show a plain win-percent on each game card
site.header: Show a plain win-percent on each game card
site.why: Turn the margin call into a clear percent chance that each team wins.

site.name: Add player attribute cards and a what-if lineup lab
site.header: Add player attribute cards and a what-if lineup lab
site.why: Glanceable player identity plus an interactive lineup explorer.

site.name: Add a report-archive browser and disclosure-window tracker
site.header: Add a report-archive browser and disclosure-window tracker
site.why: Browse past reports and track when each call was locked in.

site.name: Add a loud stale badge to the live agent strip
site.header: Add a loud stale badge to the live agent strip
site.why: Mirror the red stale warning and alert if the build process dies.

site.name: Upgrade the accuracy-transparency page
site.why: An honest us-versus-market visual, the credibility centerpiece.

floor.label: 69 lined up — a healthy runway (we keep at least 40 ready)

upcoming.note: These are the next things the system will work on — what each will be and why it matters. Tap to move an item to the top, bump it up, push it down, or shelf it. Items waiting on your call are parked until you answer — then they jump to the top.

coming_soon.header: Reply to alerts right in Discord
coming_soon.why: your Discord message becomes the decision — no tapping through to a form
coming_soon.note: needs a ~5-min one-time bot setup when it reaches the front of the queue.

reports.placeholder: Tonight's game reports land at 5:15pm ET.

rollover.label: rollover armed for tomorrow ✓
rollover.note: After 4am ET the board flips to the new day, files yesterday's reports under a dated header, and shows tonight's placeholder until the reports land at 5:15pm ET.
